lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The break room at this hour is the quietest place in the building. Not silent — the refrigerator compressor, the strip light, the distant percussion of a gurney somewhere past the double doors — but quiet in the way that matters. Nobody needs anything from him here.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The chart is open on the table.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza has read it six times. He stopped reading it two times ago and has been sitting with what he knows instead.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Thirty years. That is what the man across from him in the corridor carries. Thirty years of people who owe him something, thirty years of files he has touched and committees he has shaped and residents whose references he signed. The institution does not just protect him. The institution is, in some structural sense, partly him.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: And Reza has a chart with the wrong name on it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He closes it carefully. Squares the corners. Sets it flat on the table like something that might detonate.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The question is not whether the chart is enough. The question is what enough costs when the person you are presenting it to wrote the room you are standing in.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The shift runs on without him.
